APPLE INC.
FORM 10-K
For the fiscal year ended September 30, 2023

ITEM 1. BUSINESS

Apple Inc. ("Apple" or the "Company") designs, manufactures and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables and accessories, and sells a variety of related services. The Company's fiscal year is the 52 or 53-week period that ends on the last Saturday of September.

ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS

The Company's business, financial condition, operating results and prospects can be affected by a number of factors, whether currently known or unknown, including but not limited to those described below.

ITEM 7. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

FISCAL YEAR 2023 HIGHLIGHTS

Total net sales decreased 3% or $11.0 billion during 2023 compared to 2022. The weakness in foreign currencies relative to the U.S. dollar had an unfavorable year-over-year impact of approximately $6.3 billion or 2 percentage points on total net sales.

Products net sales decreased $13.3 billion or 7% during 2023 compared to 2022.
Services net sales increased $2.3 billion or 5% during 2023 compared to 2022.

Net sales: $383,285 million (2023), $394,328 million (2022)
Gross margin: $169,148 million (2023), $170,782 million (2022)
Operating income: $114,301 million (2023), $119,437 million (2022)
Net income: $96,995 million (2023), $99,803 million (2022)
Earnings per share — diluted: $6.13 (2023), $6.11 (2022)
